excerpts.excerpt:
CASH
Maybe I did.
He releases the clip and checks the load ... Seems
fine. He slaps it back and holsters it.
INT. SQUAD ROOM
Cash pushes through the swinging doors and into the
generalized commotion of the squad room.
He snakes his way through the station and over to an
interrogation room. Just as he's about to go in,
CAPTAIN HOLMES, black, stocky, graying hair, exits.
CASH
You get anything out of him,
Captain?
HOLMES
Nothing. He doesn't speak any
English. We had an interpreter
in there, but he said he doesn't
want to say anything until his
lawyer gets here.
CASH
Let me question him.
Holmes rubs his hands through his hair as he shakes his
head.

EXT. FIRE ESCAPE
The window gives and Cash noiselessly slides it open ...
INT. SECOND-FLOOR HALLWAY
Tango moves to the end and rounds the corner ...
HIS POV
Another long, empty corridor. The Courier has dis­
appeared again ... But then, halfway down the corridor,
an open DOOR CREAKS slightly ...
BACK TO SCENE
Tango's eyes lock on it.
INT. ROOM
Cash has just slipped in through the window. It's pitch
black except for a beam of light slanting in through
the crack in the door.
EX'l'. HALLWAY
Tango slalks to the door ...
INT. ROOM
Cash hears FOOTSTEPS in the hallway ... Swiftly takes up
a position behind the door.
EXT. HALLWAY
Tango stands right at the door. Takes out a second .45,
holds the business end against the wood door.

WYLER
I want these two turned over to
federal custody.
Shroeder pulls Holmes aside as Garcia and Wyler continue
to argue in the b.g.
SHROEDER
What do they got?
Holmes takes a deep breath. It looks bad.
HOLMES
A 9 millimeter with Cash's prints
on it. Ballistics is running a
check right now. It's the murder
weapon. They were both found on
the scene. No one else seen going
in or out.
SHROEDER
What else?
81 ANGLE INCLUDING DOOR
A federal AGENT pokes his head in.
AGENT
(to Wyler)
We're ready with the tape, sir.
Shroeder reacts. What tape?

BRIEF CUT - CASH
CASH
(under his breath)
Lying sack of shit.
PROSECUTOR
No more questions.
Tango's defense attorney gets up from the table. He's a
young go-getter type. Undoubtedly Ivy League. His name
is NOLAN.
He walks over to the witness stand.
NOLAN
Officer Davis, you've testified
that you saw Detective Tango enter
the building from the parking lot
and that you saw Detective Cash
enter from a fire escape on the
second floor. Is that correct?
DAVIS
Yeah, that's right.

Nolan sits back down at the table.
NOLAN
Ray, if I were on that jury, I'd
have to find you guilty. I'd have
no other choice.
TANGO
So you're telling me to cop a
plea?
NOLAN
There's one other option. As your
attorney I feel obligated to
discuss it with you ... You're not
going to like it.
TANGO
What is it?
NOLAN
According to the evidence you
don't appear to be the trigger
man. Cash was the lead player.
If you were to turn evidence on
him, I could maybe get you off
with no jail time at all.
Tango glares at him. It's not even a consideration.

TANGO
If we cop a plea, we do it
together.
CASH
What you're saying is you don't
want to make a deal and leave me
holding the bag. Very generous.
Like it or
together.
don't want
with you.
TANGO
not we're in this one
After the trial, I
to have anything to do
Tango
CASH
Suits me.
TANGO
Before we change our plea, I'm
going to make a statement.
CASH
I want to give a statement.

They feel the van jerk to a halt.
The back door suddenly opens. Light pours in.
OUTSIDE
Cash and Tango emerge from the van. Shackled. Squint­
ing as their eyes adjust to the sunlight. The look
around ...
PULL BACK FAST TO an EAGLE'S-EYE VIEW.
The van is parked just inside the guarded gate of an
enormous gray, factory-like building. A forty foot wall
surrounds it. Guard towers. Sharp-shooters.
This is no minimum security prison. This is the fucking
penitentiary.
ANOTHER ANGLE
Cash is in shock. Tango peruses their new surroundings
cynically.
TANGO
(dryly)
Where's the golf course?
INT. SHOWERS - DAY
Cash standing beneath the spray, ranting.
CASH
Somebody bribed the fucking prison
board, that's how we got here.

Tango puts a hand on Face's chest and shoves him back on
his heels.
Face's expression hardens to stone. He snaps his fin­
gers. The twelve inmates instantly surround Cash and
Tango.
Cash and Tango instinctively position themselves back to
back preparing for the onslaught.
A tense beat. Knives glint. CHAINS RATTLE. The THWACK
of a pipe against a palm.
CASH
You didn't have to push him.
Someone shrieks a battlecry and the inmates rush forward!
Cash jumps staright up, grabs an overhead pipe which runs
the length of the ceiling.
Crunch! The first two inmates to arrive each get a foot
to the face.
Cash swings off the pipe and lands on top of a huge
washing machine.
Four other inmates including Face charge at Tango. One
swings a lead pipe. Tango ducks. Clobbers the guy in
the stomach. The inmate collapses. Face lashes out with
a chain. Whoosh! It catches Tango in the side. He
lurches back against a huge clothes dryer. The door pops
open. The inmates move toward him.

INT. FACTORY BASEMENT
Cash bends back the grate enough to slip through. He
picks up the flashlight and disappears into the darkness.
INSIDE DUCT
Cash's flashlight burns a hole in the darkness. He can
smell the rotting concrete. The only sounds are his
footsteps and his heart thumping in his ears. He moves
forward tentatively, waving the flashlight from side to
side. Nothing to see ... Just darkness and circular walls
of cracked concrete.
Up ahead, a light glints back at him. He freezes ... Then
realizes it's a reflection. He moves closer and dis­
covers what the reflection is from ...
HIS POV
A giant propeller-like fan. Four huge blades fanning out
like wings. Filling the tunnel. Now we understand what
that ROAR coming from the other vent was.
CASH
barely manages to squeeze through the space between the
idle blades.
About ten paces beyond the fan is a round, open, steel­
covered door, suggesting the tunnel had been considered
for some other function like a fallout shelter.

CASH
Yeah.
Owen thinks.
OWEN
That weird, little guy that the
cops send all their stuff to get
cleaned up. He's got a real
state-of-the-art place on Melrose.
Has one of those funny goatee
beards.
(searching his
memory)
What's his name?
Cash suddenly realizes.
CASH
Skinner.
EXT. RODEO DRIVE - AFTERNOON
The facade of an upscale men's store. Beautifully
dressed mannequins in the display window.

INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT 201
All the lights are out. Cash is laying on the couch.
Staring at the ceiling. MUSIC is drifting into the room
from somewhere on the street.
INSIDE KIKI'S BEDROOM
She's awake. Laying on her bed. Wearing a white night­
gown. She hears the MUSIC, too. She turns longingly
toward the closed door. Thinking about Cash.
IN LIVING ROOM
Cash's eyes turn toward the hallway. Thinking about
Kiki.
INTERCUT
Kiki's hand. Gently running down the front of her night­
gown. Between her breasts.
Cash. A bead of perspiration rolling down his chest.
Kiki's lips. Her tongue running across her perfect white
teeth.
Cash clutching his pillow. Holding it over his face.
Kiki rising from her bed. Going to the mirror. Brushing
back her long hair with her fingers.
Cash rising from the couch. Gravitating toward the
hallway.
Kiki turning away from the mirror. Drawn toward the door.
The moonlight making her nightgown translucent. She
looks fantastic.

Cash disregards him and coasts the motorcycle to the back
of the four-wheler. He hits a lever and the tailgate
WHIRS down to form a ramp.
There's a BUZZING noise O.S. Owen hops out of the four­
wheeler and hurries over to the computer center. Quite
an impressive setup.
CLOSEUP - LASER PRINTER
spits out a picture of Perret and a short dossier.
OWEN
takes them back to Cash and Tango.
OWEN
Here it is.
He hands the papers to Cash who has just finished loading
the motorcycle into the four-wheeler.
Cash looks at the picture, then passes it into the front
seat to Tango. Cash scans the dossier.
CASH
This guy is a real sweetheart.
Former mercenary. Several guest
appearances at your famous
unofficial wars. Tried to sign up
with the C.I.A. Was rejected as
being 'untrustworthy' ... Not the
kind of guy you'd want to marry
your sister.
